title: Same cart, same street
summary: An old donkey-cart fruit seller in Cairo refuses to change his route, and his customers don't want him to.
body:
Mahmoud sells fruit from a donkey cart in Cairo. He is sixty-seven. His cart is the same cart his father used. The donkey is the seventh one.

His route is fixed. He starts at six in the morning at the corner near the small mosque. He stops at the bakery. He stops at the doctor's gate. He stops in front of the building with three balconies. He finishes at noon at the square near the school. He has done this route every weekday for forty-two years.

A young man from the city office came last summer. He told Mahmoud, 'Your route is not efficient. You should go to the new neighbourhood.'

Mahmoud listened. He gave the man a small orange. He said, 'My customers are old. The woman in the building with three balconies cannot come down. She lowers a basket from her window. If I change my route, she has no fruit. She is eighty-nine.'

The young man tried again. 'But the new neighbourhood has young families. More money.'

Mahmoud said, 'Young families have cars. They go to the supermarket. The old woman has only her basket.'

The young man left. He did not come back.

Mahmoud still goes the same way. The basket from the third floor still comes down on a long rope. Inside there is a small piece of paper. Last week it said: 'Two apples, three oranges, one mango if not too expensive.'

Mahmoud put in all three. He did not charge for the mango.

annotations.meaningVi: mỗi ngày trong tuần (không tính cuối tuần)
thả một cái rổ xuống — 'lower' = hạ xuống
con thứ bảy (con lừa thứ bảy)

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for duration of a routine: 'has done … for'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: A2 dùng present perfect với 'for + khoảng thời gian' để diễn tả thói quen kéo dài từ quá khứ đến hiện tại. Trong bài: 'He has done this route every weekday for forty-two years.' Câu này có nghĩa Mahmoud bắt đầu cách đây 42 năm và VẪN làm. Cấu trúc: chủ ngữ + has/have + V3 + tân ngữ + for + thời lượng. Khác với 'He did this route for forty-two years' (quá khứ đơn), câu này gợi việc đã kết thúc.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: A2 uses the present perfect with 'for + duration' for routines that continue to now. From the passage: 'He has done this route every weekday for forty-two years.' Mahmoud started 42 years ago and STILL does it. Structure: subject + has/have + past participle + object + for + duration. Compare with 'He did this route for forty-two years' (past simple), which suggests it is finished.

translationVi:
Mahmoud bán trái cây bằng xe lừa kéo ở Cairo. Ông sáu mươi bảy tuổi. Chiếc xe là chiếc xe cha ông từng dùng. Con lừa thì không phải con lừa cũ. Con lừa thứ bảy.

Lộ trình của ông cố định. Ông bắt đầu lúc sáu giờ sáng ở góc gần nhà thờ Hồi giáo nhỏ. Rồi ông rẽ trái vào phố Tarek. Ông dừng ở tiệm bánh. Ông dừng ở cổng nhà bác sĩ. Ông dừng trước toà nhà có ba ban công. Ông kết thúc lúc trưa ở quảng trường gần trường học. Ông đã đi lộ trình này mỗi ngày thường suốt bốn mươi hai năm.

Một người trẻ từ văn phòng thành phố đến mùa hè năm ngoái. Anh ta nói với Mahmoud: 'Lộ trình của bác không hiệu quả. Bác nên đi sang khu mới. Ở đó nhiều người hơn.'

Mahmoud lắng nghe. Ông đưa anh ta một quả cam nhỏ. Ông nói: 'Khách của tôi đều lớn tuổi. Họ không đi xa được. Bà cụ trong toà nhà ba ban công không xuống được. Bà thả rổ từ cửa sổ. Nếu tôi đổi lộ trình, bà không có trái cây. Bà tám mươi chín.'

Người trẻ thử lại: 'Nhưng khu mới có nhiều gia đình trẻ. Tiền nhiều hơn.'

Mahmoud nói: 'Gia đình trẻ có ô tô. Họ đi siêu thị. Bà cụ chỉ có cái rổ của bà.'

Người trẻ đi. Anh ta không quay lại.

Mahmoud vẫn đi lối cũ. Cái rổ từ tầng ba vẫn xuống bằng sợi dây dài. Trong rổ có một mẩu giấy nhỏ. Tuần trước nó ghi: 'Hai quả táo, ba quả cam, một quả xoài nếu không quá đắt.'

Mahmoud bỏ vào hai quả táo, ba quả cam và một quả xoài. Ông không tính tiền quả xoài.

questions.prompt: What does Mahmoud sell, and how?
questions.explanation: 'Mahmoud sells fruit from a donkey cart in Cairo.'

options: A new cart from this year.
questions.explanation: 'His cart is the same cart his father used.'

questions.prompt: Where does Mahmoud start his route?
options: At the corner near the small mosque, at six in the morning.
At the square near the school.

questions.prompt: How long has Mahmoud been doing this route?
What did the young man from the city office say?
options: Mahmoud's route is not efficient; he should move to the new neighbourhood.

questions.prompt: How does the woman on the third floor get her fruit?
options: She lowers a basket from her window on a long rope.
She walks down with a bag.
questions.explanation: 'She lowers a basket from her window.'

questions.prompt: How old is the old woman?
Why does Mahmoud think young families don't need him?
options: They have cars and go to the supermarket.
questions.explanation: 'Young families have cars. They go to the supermarket.'

questions.prompt: What did last week's paper in the basket say?
options: 'Two apples, three oranges, one mango if not too expensive.'

questions.prompt: What did Mahmoud do about the mango?
options: He put it in the basket but did not charge for it.
He left it out because it was too expensive.
He sent a different fruit instead.
questions.explanation: 'He did not charge for the mango.'

options: A 'less efficient' route is the right route because it serves people who have nowhere else to go.
Old donkey carts are better than trucks.
questions.explanation: Mahmoud's defence of his route and the closing mango.

cloze.template:
Mahmoud sells fruit from a donkey {{b1}} in Cairo. He is sixty-seven. His cart is the same cart his father used. The donkey is the {{b2}} one.

His route is fixed. He starts at six in the morning at the corner near the small {{b3}}. He stops at the bakery. He stops at the doctor's gate. He stops in front of the building with three {{b4}}. He finishes at noon at the square near the school. He has done this route every weekday for {{b5}} years.

A young man from the city office came last summer. He told Mahmoud, 'Your route is not {{b6}}. You should go to the new neighbourhood.'

Mahmoud listened. He gave the man a small orange. He said, 'My customers are old. The woman in the building with three balconies cannot come down. She {{b7}} a basket from her window. If I change my route, she has no fruit. She is eighty-nine.'

The young man tried again. 'But the new neighbourhood has young families. More money.'

Mahmoud said, 'Young families have cars. They go to the {{b8}}. The old woman has only her basket.'

The young man left. He did not come back.

Mahmoud still goes the same way. The basket from the third floor still comes down on a long {{b9}}. Inside there is a small piece of paper. Last week it said: 'Two apples, three oranges, one mango if not too expensive.'

Mahmoud put in all three. He did not {{b10}} for the mango.

blanks.explanation: Did not charge for the mango.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The young official measures Mahmoud's route by efficiency. Mahmoud measures it by who would be left without fruit if he changed. When have you been pressured to abandon an 'inefficient' commitment that was actually load-bearing for someone else?
